     `npm run scoreboard -- --write` to refresh it. -->

# scoreboard

What pi-vim's own harnesses report about it: how closely it tracks real nvim,
what it costs a Pi process, and how big it ships. Every number below is
generated — `npm run scoreboard` reprints this document from the suites
themselves.

| | |
| --- | --- |
| measured | 2026-07-21 |
| node | v24.15.0 |
| nvim | NVIM v0.12.4 |
| platform | darwin-arm64 |
| cpu | Apple M2 Pro |

Timings are machine-dependent, and the startup figure is fragile enough that
the performance section spells out how to read it. Parity counts and the
package footprint are neither — they reproduce anywhere.

## parity against real nvim

Each case drives headless nvim and pi-vim through the same keystrokes from the
same buffer, then compares text, cursor, mode, and the unnamed register. A
row's skips are documented divergences, listed in full below — never
silently-passing tests.

| suite | pass | fail | skip |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| nvim parity char-find motions | 18 | 0 | 0 |
| nvim parity dot repeat | 31 | 0 | 0 |
| nvim parity single-key edits and replace | 15 | 0 | 2 |
| nvim parity line, buffer, and vertical motions | 14 | 0 | 6 |
| nvim parity linewise operators | 12 | 0 | 5 |
| nvim parity mode switching | 9 | 0 | 0 |
| nvim parity operators with motions | 41 | 0 | 0 |
| nvim parity put + nvim parity joins | 11 | 0 | 2 |
| nvim parity structural motions | 12 | 0 | 4 |
| nvim parity text objects | 14 | 0 | 0 |
| nvim parity visual mode | 76 | 0 | 5 |
| nvim parity word motions | 12 | 0 | 0 |
| nvim parity smoke + nvim parity regressions | 8 | 0 | 0 |
| **total** | **273** | **0** | **24** |

### known gaps

Every skipped case, verbatim from the harness. Each one is a place pi-vim
knowingly differs from nvim; `README.md` explains the user-visible ones.

**nvim parity single-key edits and replace**

- S changes the current line linewise
- D deletes through EOL and backs cursor onto last remaining char

**nvim parity line, buffer, and vertical motions**

- 9l: clamps at the final character
- gg: moves to the first line with nvim cursor placement
- G: moves to the last line with nvim cursor placement
- 2gg: moves to the counted line with nvim cursor placement
- 2G: moves to the counted line with nvim cursor placement
- j: clamps to the last character on a shorter target line

**nvim parity linewise operators**

- cc changes the current line
- 2cc changes two lines with a prefix count
- c2c changes two lines with an operator count
- c_ changes the current line linewise
- y2k yanks the current line through two lines above

**nvim parity put + nvim parity joins**

- p puts a line-wise register below the current line
- P puts a line-wise register above the current line

**nvim parity structural motions**

- }: moves from paragraph text to the next paragraph boundary
- 2}: moves across two paragraph boundaries
- {: moves from paragraph text to the previous paragraph boundary
- 2{: moves across two paragraph boundaries

**nvim parity visual mode**

- vD deletes the touched line
- vX deletes the touched line
- vjD deletes both touched lines
- v}d deletes through the paragraph boundary
- vGd deletes through the last line

## unit tests

`npm test` covers the pure modules and `ModalEditor`'s observable behavior:
**823 pass, 0 fail, 0 skip**.

The EX-to-Pi command bridge has no parity suite by design — it is a Pi
integration surface, not a vim motion, so nvim has nothing to say about it.
Unit tests cover it alone.

## performance

From `script/perf-bench.ts`. Startup and heap ask what pi-vim adds to a Pi
process; responsiveness asks how long `handleInput` takes to turn a keystroke
into an edit. Rendering is excluded throughout.

### startup

Median and range over 7 cold process launches. `index.ts` imports the Pi host,
so the last two rows share almost all of their work.

| stage | median | min–max |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| node runtime only | 81.5 ms | 79.0–82.9 ms |
| Pi host import | 644.3 ms | 635.2–670.6 ms |
| + pi-vim import | 678.6 ms | 668.7–693.2 ms |

Subtracting those two medians gives **34.2 ms** for pi-vim's own share, but
the host import's own run-to-run spread is **35.4 ms** — wider than the gap
itself — and the extension import is measured second, on caches the host
import warmed. Treat it as an upper bound rather than a measurement; on a
loaded machine the same subtraction comes out negative.

### memory

Median `heapUsed` right after import, over 5 runs with gc forced.

| stage | median heap | min–max |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Pi host import | 45946.0 KiB | 45943.0–45954.8 KiB |
| + pi-vim import | 48769.9 KiB | 48760.2–48773.0 KiB |
| **pi-vim's own heap** | **2823.9 KiB** | — |

Unlike startup, this difference is resolvable: it is 222× the widest
run-to-run spread of either row (12.7 KiB).

### responsiveness

Time spent inside `handleInput`. A *keystroke* row times one key; a *command*
row times every key of the command, count digits included.

| operation | per | median | p95 |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: |
| `h` one column left, on a 4k-column line | keystroke | 449.76 µs | 452.59 µs |
| an unbound printable key in normal mode (no-op) | keystroke | 0.73 µs | 0.74 µs |
| `10w` across a 400-word line | command | 37.34 µs | 39.23 µs |
| `3fX` across a 600-column line | command | 38.52 µs | 40.34 µs |
| `200j` down a 320-line buffer | command | 3.22 µs | 3.57 µs |
| `50p` char-wise put of a yanked word | command | 2018.90 µs | 2019.48 µs |
| `w` on a 20-word line | keystroke | 1.15 µs | 1.19 µs |
| `b` on a 20-word line | keystroke | 1.28 µs | 1.76 µs |
| `w` on a 50-word line | keystroke | 1.37 µs | 1.38 µs |
| `b` on a 50-word line | keystroke | 1.73 µs | 1.80 µs |
| `w` on a 100-word line | keystroke | 1.76 µs | 1.77 µs |
| `b` on a 100-word line | keystroke | 1.95 µs | 2.09 µs |
| `w` on a 200-word line | keystroke | 2.64 µs | 2.71 µs |
| `b` on a 200-word line | keystroke | 2.96 µs | 2.98 µs |
| `w` on a 400-word line | keystroke | 4.76 µs | 4.80 µs |
| `b` on a 400-word line | keystroke | 5.06 µs | 5.46 µs |
| `dw` on a 400-word line | command | 9.32 µs | 9.89 µs |
| `yw` on a 400-word line | command | 4.36 µs | 4.57 µs |

The costliest single keystroke is `h` one column left, on a 4k-column line, at
450 µs; the costliest whole command is `50p` char-wise put of a yanked word,
at 2019 µs. Both scale with the text they walk — the `w` and `b` ladders from
20 to 400 words isolate that scaling.

## published footprint

From `npm pack --dry-run`, against the budgets `npm run pack:check` enforces
on every commit. Tests and docs are excluded from the package, so only shipped
code and `README.md` count.

| measure | actual | budget | headroom |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| files | 17 | 17 | 0 |
| packed size | 48,885 B | 49,400 B | 515 B |
| unpacked size | 200,117 B | 200,600 B | 483 B |

## reproducing this

```sh
npm ci
npm test           # unit tests
npm run test:nvim  # parity suites (needs nvim on PATH)
npm run pack:check # published footprint vs budgets
npm run scoreboard # regenerate this document
```
